 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.4.6","title":"CI: PSScriptAnalyzer for install.ps1","description":"# Goal
Add static analysis for the Windows installer script (`install.ps1`) so it doesn't silently rot.

---

# What to run
- Install `PSScriptAnalyzer`
- Run `Invoke-ScriptAnalyzer -Path install.ps1` with a strict severity gate

---

# Reference patterns
- `cass` supports a PowerShell installer; our Windows path should be equally maintainable.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- Windows CI job runs ScriptAnalyzer.
- CI fails on high severity findings.
- Output is actionable (line numbers + rule names).
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:42:42.884078024-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T03:32:53.385728391-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T03:32:53.385728391-05:00","close_reason":"Added installer-lint-pwsh CI job (PSScriptAnalyzer) for install.ps1","labels":["ci","github-actions","installer","windows"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.4.6","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:42:42.914500788-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.5","title":"Docs: installation + troubleshooting + upgrade (operator-first)","description":"# Goal
Write **operator-first docs** for installing and verifying demonstrateable BrennerBot toolchain.

Docs should answer:
- “What do I run on a fresh machine?”
- “How do I confirm it worked?”
- “How do I upgrade safely?”
- “What breaks on Windows / without tmux?”

# Required sections
1. **Quick Install (recommended)**
   - `curl | bash -s -- --easy-mode --verify`
2. **Verification**
   - `brenner doctor`
   - `ntm deps -v`
   - `cass health`
   - `cm onboard status`
3. **Troubleshooting**
   - PATH not updated
   - missing tmux (ntm)
   - corporate proxy / SSL issues
4. **Upgrade**
   - `brenner upgrade` (if implemented)
   - re-run installer with `--version` pinned
5. **Security notes**
   - checksums
   - pinned versions (toolchain manifest)

# Acceptance criteria
- README includes a prominent install section.
- A dedicated runbook exists (or README section is sufficient) with copy/paste commands.
- Documentation is aligned with the installer flags and CI/release behavior.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:37:28.43930199-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T22:37:28.43930199-05:00","labels":["docs","installer","ops","runbook"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:37:28.440389501-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:54:57.54996306-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:54:57.607993951-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:54:57.643118877-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.5.1","title":"Docs: README quick install + verification snippet","description":"# Goal
Update `README.md` to include the canonical **operator install** commands and verification steps.

---

# Requirements
- Show the “happy path” one-liner:
  - `curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash -s -- --easy-mode --verify`
- Show verification commands:
  - `brenner doctor`
  - `ntm deps -v`
  - `cass health`
  - `cm onboard status`

Also include a pinned-tag install option once releases exist.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- README contains a dedicated Install section with copy/paste commands.
- README instructions match the actual installer flags.

-{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.5.2","title":"Docs: troubleshooting + upgrade runbook (PATH, tmux, proxies, Windows)","description":"# Goal
Write a self-contained troubleshooting runbook for bootstrap installs.

---

# Topics to cover
- PATH not updated (zsh/bash/fish)
- macOS Gatekeeper prompts (if we ship unsigned binaries)
- missing `tmux` (ntm dependency) + how to install
- corporate proxy / SSL issues with `curl` + GitHub
- Windows quirks:
  - where `install.ps1` installs
  - how PATH update works
  - why `ntm` is skipped

# Upgrade story
- “upgrade via re-running installer”
- `brenner upgrade` (once implemented)
- toolchain manifest pinning and how to bump versions intentionally

---

# Acceptance criteria
- A new operator can recover from common failures without reading source.
- Runbook aligns with actual installer flags and `brenner doctor` output.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.6","title":"Repo hygiene: ignore generated dirs (node_modules, coverage, playwright)","description":"# Goal
Prevent accidental commits and tooling noise from generated directories.

# Background
- This repo currently has a minimal `.gitignore` and generated directories commonly appear in working trees (`node_modules`, Playwright reports, coverage, Next build output, etc.).
- This increases risk of accidentally committing artifacts and also makes tools like `ubs` noisy when scanning the repo.

# Current risk
`git ls-files` shows at least one tracked file under `node_modules/` (likely a generated Vitest cache file).

Per AGENTS.md, removing it from git requires a destructive git operation; I need explicit user-provided approval before I can run or propose the exact command.

# Scope
- Expand `.gitignore` with common generated directories and files:
  - `node_modules/`
  - `apps/web/node_modules/`
  - `apps/web/.next/`
  - `apps/web/playwright-report/`
  - `apps/web/test-results/`
  - `apps/web/coverage/`
  - `dist/`
  - `*.tsbuildinfo`

# Acceptance criteria
- `.gitignore` updates prevent new generated artifacts from being staged by default.
- Follow-up is documented for cleaning the already-tracked `node_modules/` artifact (requires explicit approval).
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurplePond","created_at":"2025-12-30T08:13:59.272715697-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T08:17:32.126789192-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T08:17:32.126789192-05:00","close_reason":"Expanded .gitignore to exclude generated dirs (node_modules, .next, coverage, playwright reports) and non-bun lockfiles; verified apps/web/public/_corpus is not ignored. NOTE: 1 tracked file remains under node_modules/ and will require explicit user-approved untracking.","labels":["ci","git","repo-hygiene"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.6","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T08:13:59.284129176-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.2","title":"EPIC: Corpus Engine (parse/search/quote-bank/excerpts)","description":"# Goal
Make the corpus usable as a *tool*, not just a document:
- fast full-text search across the transcript + distillations
- stable anchors (`§n`) and excerpt composition
- a quote bank tagged by operators/primitives

# Why this matters
The corpus is the "ground truth". If it’s hard to search, cite, and excerpt, the whole system drifts into ungrounded synthesis.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:21:57.346833589-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T18:21:57.346833589-05:00","labels":["corpus","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:21:57.347703186-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.2.1","title":"Corpus: transcript section parser + structured index","description":"# Goal
Turn `complete_brenner_transcript.md` into a structured representation (sections keyed by `§n`) usable by:
- search
- excerpt composition
- citation links

# Acceptance criteria
- A parser can produce `{sectionId, title, content}` entries for all sections.
- Output is stable and deterministic.
